title: socket-wheelhouse per-repo config
description: Per-repo socket-wheelhouse config. Two valid locations: `.config/socket-wheelhouse.json` (primary) or `.socket-wheelhouse.json` at the repo root (alternative). Both are first-class — pick the location that fits your repo's convention.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocompletion. Conventionally `./socket-wheelhouse-schema.json` — both the config and its schema live side-by-side in `.config/`.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes; readers gate on it.

repoName.description: Canonical repo basename (e.g. `socket-lib`, `ultrathink`). Used for layout / native-independent exemptions like the oxlint `socket-lib` carve-out.

layout.description: Package layout. `single-package` = one `package.json` at root, no `packages/`. `monorepo` = pnpm workspaces under `packages/`.

native.description: Native-binary supply-chain role. `none` = pure-npm publish path. `consumer` = pulls prebuilt binaries from a sibling producer. `producer` = ships native artifacts via GH releases. `both` = consumes one set, produces another. (Per-language ports live in `lockstep.json` `lang-parity` rows, not here.)

kind.description: Build/run character (drives CI breadth), orthogonal to `native`. `node-runner` = the repo runs on Node; its checks/tests are OS-independent, so CI runs lean (Linux-only by default). `native-builder` = the repo compiles native artifacts on a build server (heavy toolchain image, multi-OS/arch matrix; socket-btm). Optional — defaults to `node-runner`. NOTE: orthogonal to `native` — socket-cli is `native: consumer` yet `kind: node-runner` (it pulls prebuilt binaries; its own CI just runs Node).

capabilities.description: Repo capabilities — traits the repo HAS that fleet tooling keys off (NOT specific to any one tool). Each KEY is a lowercase trait token; each VALUE is an array of package globs (repo-relative) saying WHERE in the repo the trait applies. Repo-wide = `["."]`. Consumers: off-by-default hooks gate on key-presence (a hook fires on commands anywhere in the repo, so it only needs to know the trait exists); capability-specific lint rules + build/CI lanes scope to the globs (e.g. run clippy only under the cargo packages). Keys are enum-constrained so a typo fails validation instead of silently gating nothing.

cargo.description: Repo builds Rust via `cargo build --release` (or a fleet build-prod alias). Value = package globs containing the Rust/cargo source (e.g. `["packages/*-builder"]`). Gates concurrent-cargo-build-guard (key-presence) today; future cargo/clippy lint rules + rust build/CI lanes scope to these globs.

enablePrePush.description: Wire `.git-hooks/pre-push` (shell shim) → `.git-hooks/pre-push.mts`. Mandatory security gate; default true.

enableCommitMsg.description: Wire `.git-hooks/commit-msg` (shell shim) → `.git-hooks/commit-msg.mts`. Strips AI attribution; default true.

enablePreCommit.description: Wire `.git-hooks/pre-commit` (shell shim) → `.git-hooks/pre-commit.mts`. Lint + secret scan on staged files; default true.

preCommitVariant.description: `lint-only` runs format + secret scan; `lint-test` adds vitest on touched packages. Default `lint-test`.

required.description: Override REQUIRED_SCRIPTS from manifest.mts. Usually omitted — the fleet default applies.

optional.description: Per-script opt-in map keyed by script name. `true` = repo ships this RECOMMENDED script; `false` = explicit opt-out.

bodyExempt.description: Script names whose body is allowed to drift from the canonical form (e.g. socket-lib runs a richer test runner than the standard `node scripts/fleet/test.mts`). Each entry is the script name only.

profile.description: `standard` requires the fleet plugin set (import + typescript + unicorn). `rich` opts into a wider set; check the runner for the exact basenames currently exempted.

provenance.description: Repo publishes with npm provenance (OIDC). Hint for setup helpers; not enforced by the checker today.

requirePinnedFullSha.description: Enforce 40-char SHA pins on every `uses:` ref. Defaults to true; an opt-out is reserved for special cases (e.g. workflow-dispatch test rigs) and currently has no consumer.

testOs.description: OS breadth for the reusable ci.yml TEST job (check/lint/type are always Linux-only). `all` (default) runs ubuntu+macos+windows; `linux` runs ubuntu only. Opt into `linux` ONLY for code with no OS-sensitive behavior (e.g. a pure HTTP/string lib). Repos with path/fs/spawn/platform branches (socket-lib, socket-registry) MUST stay `all` — Linux-only would hide Windows regressions. The caller `ci.yml` reads this to set `os-versions`.

capabilities.description: Repo capabilities that gate off-by-default fleet hooks. Each is a trait the repo HAS (e.g. `cargo` = builds Rust via cargo), not a hook name — the wheelhouse `CAPABILITY_HOOKS` map decides which hooks each capability installs. Declared here once; cascade installs the gated hooks + wires settings.json only for repos that list the capability. Enum-constrained so a typo fails validation instead of silently no-op-ing.

anyOf.description: Repo builds Rust via `cargo build --release` (or a fleet build-prod alias). Installs `concurrent-cargo-build-guard`, which blocks a second release build while one is in flight.

includeSharedSkills.description: Ship `.claude/skills/fleet/_shared/*` — env-check, path-guard-rule, report-format, security-tools, verify-build.

includeUpdatingSkill.description: Ship the dependency-update skill. Reserved — no consumer wired today.

workspace.description: pnpm-workspace.yaml setting hints. The runner reads from the YAML; this block exists for repos that prefer to declare intent in JSON.

allowBuilds.description: pnpm `onlyBuiltDependencies` allowlist. Map a package name to true/false to grant/deny build scripts.

blockExoticSubdeps.description: Refuse transitive git/tarball subdeps (direct git deps still allowed). Required true; the field exists so a repo can document the intent locally.

minimumReleaseAge.description: Soak time in minutes before installing freshly-published packages. Fleet default 10080 (= 7 days).

minimumReleaseAgeExclude.description: Scopes / package patterns exempt from the soak time. Socket-owned scopes typically listed here.

apps.description: GitHub App slugs that must be installed on the repo (e.g. `cursor`, `socket-security`, `socket-trufflehog`). Audited by `scripts/fleet/lint-github-settings.mts` — apps whose installation cannot be reliably detected via check-suites are trusted via this manifest.

pathsAllowlist.description: Exemptions for the path-hygiene gate (scripts/fleet/check/paths-are-canonical.mts). Each entry needs a `reason`; prefer narrow entries (rule + file + snippet_hash + pattern) over blanket file-level exempts.

items.description: One exemption for the path-hygiene gate.

rule.description: Rule letter (A, B, C, D, F, G). Omit to match any rule.

file.description: Substring match against the relative file path.

pattern.description: Substring match against the offending snippet.

line.description: Exact line number. Strict — no fuzz tolerance.

snippet_hash.description: 12-char SHA-256 prefix of the normalized snippet (whitespace collapsed). Drift-resistant: keeps matching after reformatting that doesn't change the offending construction. Get via `node scripts/fleet/check/paths-are-canonical.mts --show-hashes`.

reason.description: Why this site is genuinely exempt. Required.
